problem_addressed: Semi-supervised learning algorithms struggle with imbalanced data due to pseudo-label bias toward majority classes, which gets amplified during training.
core_claim: Existing pseudo-labeling strategies using uncalibrated confidence or heuristics are unreliable for imbalanced SSL; SEVAL addresses this through statistical analysis-based optimization of refinement and thresholds.
key_contributions: SEVAL framework for imbalanced SSL using validation data
Pseudo-label refinement via logit offset optimization
Threshold adjustment based on precision rather than recall
novelty: First to prove precision (not recall) should drive threshold selection for imbalanced SSL (Lemma 4), and to use validation data to directly optimize both refinement parameters and thresholds for class-wise accuracy.
method_summary: SEVAL optimizes logit offsets π to minimize cross-entropy on validation data and learns thresholds τ to achieve target per-class accuracy, using a balanced validation subset separate from training data.
method_components: Pseudo-label refinement: π* = argmin π Σ H(yi, σ(zV_i - log π))
Threshold adjustment: τ* = argmin τc [A(τc) - t] if t < αc else 0
key_innovation: Using validation data to directly optimize for precision-based thresholds and logit offsets, overcoming the unreliability of uncalibrated model confidence in imbalanced scenarios.
assumptions: Holdout validation data V has same class-conditional distribution as training data
Class priors are uniform in validation set (PT(Y) uniform)
Access to labeled validation data separate from training/unlabeled data
theoretical_grounding: Lemma 4 proves precision > recall for optimal thresholds; Proposition 1 establishes optimal decision boundary conditions; cross-entropy minimization provides theoretical basis for π optimization.
headline_result: ReMixMatch with SEVAL: 86.7% on CIFAR-100 n1=50
improvement_over_baseline: SEVAL improves DASO by +1.0% (51.4%→52.4%) on CIFAR-100, FixMatch by +0.9% (84.4%→85.3%) on CIFAR-10
stated_limitations: The abstract doesn't explicitly state limitations of the approach
unstated_weaknesses: May not explicitly address uncertainty quantification in pseudo-labeling
Potential lack of integration with uncertainty-aware selection mechanisms
May not provide a comprehensive framework for handling uncertainty in imbalanced SSL
threats_to_validity: Without full paper details, potential threats to validity cannot be fully assessed
Limited information on how the approach handles uncertainty in pseudo-label selection
Unclear how the threshold adjustment accounts for uncertainty estimates
open_questions: How does the proposed approach integrate uncertainty quantification with pseudo-label refinement?
What is the relationship between threshold adjustment and uncertainty estimates?
How does the method handle cases where uncertainty is high but confidence is also high?
future_work: Without full paper, future work suggestions cannot be accurately identified
gaps_this_enables: The paper may identify gaps in current pseudo-label refinement approaches for imbalanced SSL
Potential for developing more uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection methods
Opportunity to integrate uncertainty quantification with threshold adjustment strategies
relevance_to_topic: This paper directly addresses pseudo-label refinement in imbalanced SSL, providing a foundation for developing uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection methods.
best_result: ReMixMatch with SEVAL: 86.7% on CIFAR-100 n1=50
limitations_stated: The abstract doesn't explicitly state limitations of the approach
title: Learning Label Refinement and Threshold Adjustment for Imbalanced Semi-Supervised Learning
